stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The tags confirm its relevance within the transformer and Llama ecosystem, a core area of AI research.

stories.title: Apple takes Epic fight over app store fees to the Supreme Court
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Apple is escalating its legal battle with Epic Games to the Supreme Court.
The core issue before the Supreme Court is whether a prior contempt finding against Apple in the Epic case was legally flawed.
This case has significant implications for app store economics and platform regulation across the tech industry.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating its primary function.
The tags suggest relevance to transformers, safetensors, and the Llama family of models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation tasks, indicating its capability in generative AI.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the mainstream LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: Faster Gemma 4 on MLX with multi-token prediction
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Gemma 4 performance is significantly boosted in Ollama 0.31 on Apple Silicon.
The speed improvement is attributed to the implementation of Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) powered by MLX.
Performance gains reach up to 90% faster when utilized with coding agents, based on the Aider benchmark.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for Apple Silicon.
The performance enhancement is achieved by leveraging MLX, Apple's native machine learning framework.
This preview signals a significant step toward optimized, on-device AI inference on Apple hardware.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
This emphasizes a trend toward private, on-device AI execution rather than cloud dependency.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It supports running various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no manual environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: New attack provides one more reason why AI browsers are a bad idea
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: New vulnerability demonstrates LLM susceptibility to prompt injection attacks.
The attack vector involves simple, seemingly innocuous inputs (e.g., '2 + 2 = 5') to bypass safety guardrails.
The finding casts significant doubt on the security and reliability of AI-integrated browsers.

stories.title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
stories.author: Arman Cohan
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical weakness in Large Language Models (LLMs): the inability to accurately express uncertainty.
The proposed solution involves integrating Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback to improve self-monitoring in LLMs.
This work aims to reduce LLM hallucinations and enhance overall trustworthiness by enabling models to recognize their own knowledge boundaries.

stories.title: AxDafny: Agentic Verified Code Generation in Dafny
stories.author: Leopoldo Sarra
bullet_points: Introduces AxDafny, a framework for agentic code generation in Dafny that requires generating both executable code and formal proof artifacts.
AxDafny utilizes a verifier-guided repair mechanism to iteratively refine implementations, invariants, assertions, and termination arguments.
Presents LiveCodeBench-Pro-Dafny (LCB-Pro-Dafny), a new benchmark of 250 competition-style problems with formal specifications for verifier-based evaluation.

stories.title: Surrogate Fidelity: When Can Open LLMs Explain Closed Ones?
stories.author: Nehal Bandi
bullet_points: The paper addresses the 'surrogate problem' in Mechanistic Interpretability (MI), where full access to proprietary LLMs is unavailable.
It investigates when measurements taken on open-source LLMs can reliably inform conclusions about closed, black-box models.
Fidelity is evaluated across prediction, attribution, and representation levels, proposing API-compatible scalar readouts like log-odds.

stories.title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
stories.author: Arman Cohan
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical weakness in LLMs: their inability to accurately express uncertainty and recognize knowledge boundaries.
It proposes using Reinforcement Learning combined with Metacognitive Feedback to improve LLM self-monitoring capabilities.
The goal is to enhance the trustworthiness and reliability of LLMs by making them better at knowing what they don't know.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings improved performance and expanded GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to enhance model support across various hardware.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes local-first operation, allowing AI to run entirely on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, integrating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced AI features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of 'subagents' for more complex task decomposition.
Web search functionality has been added to enhance the context and knowledge base of Claude Code.

stories.title: Cleared by the US, derailed by the UK: Getty’s Shutterstock merger falls apart
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Getty is abandoning its $3.7 billion merger with Shutterstock.
The deal collapsed due to restrictions imposed by a UK regulator.
This occurred despite receiving unconditional antitrust clearance from the US Department of Justice.

stories.title: Meta is adding ridiculous ‘rate limits’ and a soft paywall to its smart glasses
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Meta is implementing rate limits on its smart glasses' Conversation Focus feature.
Access to the feature will be restricted to three hours per month without a subscription.
A paid tier, Meta One Premium ($19.99/month), is required to remove these usage limits.

stories.title: Dish files for bankruptcy, but not shutting down
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Dish has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection.
The bankruptcy filing is intended to allow the company to continue operations, not shut down.
The filing is linked to delays in the sale of $23 billion in 5G spectrum to AT&T.

stories.title: Amazon fined $2.25 million for failing to help identity theft victims
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Amazon was fined $2.25 million by the Federal Trade Commission (FTC).
The fine stems from allegations that Amazon failed to assist customers who were victims of identity theft.
The FTC specifically accused Amazon of not providing customers with details regarding purchases made using fraudulent accounts.

stories.title: Google’s NotebookLM can sum up your research in a TikTok-style clip
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Google's NotebookLM is introducing a feature to generate short, TikTok-style AI videos from user-uploaded research sources.
The new capability is currently exclusive to Google AI Ultra and Pro subscribers.
This represents an integration of generative AI into personal knowledge management tools, focusing on digestible content formats.

stories.title: Reddit will require you to log in to use old.reddit.com
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Reddit is implementing a mandatory login requirement for accessing old.reddit.com.
The primary motivation for this change is to combat abusive scraping activities.
This move restricts anonymous access to historical content on the platform.

stories.title: Amazon blames piracy apps with malware for killing new Fire Stick sideloading
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Amazon is addressing malware risks associated with third-party sideloading apps on Fire Sticks.
The new Fire Stick OS update introduces features to block unauthorized third-party homepage launchers and ad blockers.
The change aims to improve security and control over the Fire Stick ecosystem by limiting external modifications.

stories.title: NASA may send a backup, nuclear-powered Mars rover to the Moon
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA is considering sending a backup nuclear-powered Mars rover to the Moon.
The concept involves repurposing advanced Mars technology for lunar exploration.
The potential capability is described as an 'awesome' advancement in space exploration.

stories.title: Google kills Tenor GIF API, forcing changes at X, Discord, and more
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google has discontinued its Tenor GIF API, impacting third-party integrations.
Platforms like X and Discord are forced to find alternative sources for GIF functionality.
Tenor maintains connectivity with Google applications, but external API access is restricted.

stories.title: Trump's plan to redesign every .gov website leads to AI-designed horrors
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Trump's initiative to overhaul all .gov websites is facing delays.
The project, managed by the National Design Studio, has postponed its update plan.
The article suggests the redesign effort risks producing poorly designed, AI-generated interfaces.

stories.title: Introspective Coupling: Self-Explanation Training Tracks Behavioral Change Despite Fixed Supervision
stories.author: Belinda Z. Li
bullet_points: The research investigates whether training Language Models (LMs) to generate explanations leads to genuine 'faithful introspection' or just superficial imitation.
The study uses counterfactual behavior on modified inputs as a novel form of supervision for the LMs.
Findings suggest that LMs can track behavioral change even when trained on fixed counterfactual explanations from past checkpoints or similar models.

stories.title: QVal: Cheaply Evaluating Dense Supervision Signals for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
stories.author: Matthias Bethge
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of sparse rewards in long-horizon LLM agent tasks.
Proposes QVal, a method for cheaply evaluating dense supervision signals for intermediate actions.
Highlights the common pitfall of evaluating dense supervision methods solely based on downstream performance.

stories.title: When LLMs Read Tables Carelessly: Measuring and Reducing Data Referencing Errors
stories.author: Huzefa Rangwala
bullet_points: LLMs exhibit data referencing errors (DREs) when processing tables, even when they grasp the table structure.
DREs undermine the reliability of intermediate reasoning steps, not just the final answer accuracy.
This research provides the first systematic, large-scale evaluation of these tabular data referencing errors.

stories.title: Freeform Preference Learning for Robotic Manipulation
stories.author: Chelsea Finn
bullet_points: Addresses the critical bottleneck of reward design in autonomous robotic manipulation.
Introduces Freeform Preference Learning (FPL) to overcome limitations of binary preference labeling.
Allows human annotators to provide nuanced feedback on robot trajectories rather than simple 'better/worse' choices.

stories.title: AdaJEPA: An Adaptive Latent World Model
stories.author: Mengye Ren
bullet_points: Introduces AdaJEPA, an adaptive latent world model designed to improve planning capabilities.
Addresses the limitation of traditional latent world models by enabling test-time adaptation within Model Predictive Control (MPC).
Aims to prevent planning failures caused by inaccurate predictions or test-time distribution shift.

stories.title: TRIAGE: Role-Typed Credit Assignment for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Alborz Geramifard
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in standard Reinforcement Learning (RL) where a single final outcome is used to assign credit to all agent actions.
TRIAGE proposes a 'role-typed credit assignment' framework to provide a more nuanced signal for agentic RL.
This framework aims to solve issues where standard methods unfairly penalize useful exploration or reinforce redundant actions.

stories.title: PolicyGuard: From Organizational Policies to Neuro-SymbolicCompliance Review Engines
stories.author: Amar Prakash Azad
bullet_points: PolicyGuard introduces a neuro-symbolic framework for policy-grounded document compliance review.
It addresses the limitation of LLMs where applied policy logic remains implicit, hindering inspection and testing.
The system converts organizational policies into a structured format for rigorous compliance checking.

stories.title: Introspective Coupling: Self-Explanation Training Tracks Behavioral Change Despite Fixed Supervision
stories.author: Belinda Z. Li
bullet_points: The research investigates how to ensure Language Models (LMs) provide faithful self-explanations rather than just superficial mimicry.
The study uses counterfactual behavior on modified inputs as a novel form of supervision for training explanatory LMs.
Surprisingly, the models showed behavioral change even when trained on fixed counterfactual explanations from earlier or similar models.

stories.title: QVal: Cheaply Evaluating Dense Supervision Signals for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
stories.author: Matthias Bethge
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of sparse rewards in long-horizon LLM agent tasks.
Proposes 'QVal' to cheaply evaluate dense supervision signals for intermediate actions.
Highlights the common pitfall of evaluating dense supervision methods solely based on downstream performance.

stories.title: Freeform Preference Learning for Robotic Manipulation
stories.author: Chelsea Finn
bullet_points: Addresses the critical bottleneck of reward design in autonomous robotic manipulation.
Introduces Freeform Preference Learning (FPL) to learn policies from human feedback.
Moves beyond binary comparisons by allowing humans to define natural, freeform preferences for robot trajectories.

stories.title: AdaJEPA: An Adaptive Latent World Model
stories.author: Mengye Ren
bullet_points: Introduces AdaJEPA, an adaptive latent world model designed to improve planning robustness.
Addresses the critical issue of prediction inaccuracy and failure during test-time distribution shift in latent world models.
Integrates test-time adaptation directly within the closed-loop framework of Model Predictive Control (MPC).

stories.title: Automated Background Swapping for Robustness against Spurious Backgrounds
stories.author: Kajetan Schweighofer
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical issue of spurious correlations in Deep Neural Networks, where models rely on non-causal features for prediction.
Specifically targets the vision domain, focusing on how background elements can introduce misleading correlations that cause catastrophic generalization failures.
Proposes an 'Automated Background Swapping' technique to enhance model robustness against these spurious background dependencies.

stories.title: TRIAGE: Role-Typed Credit Assignment for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Alborz Geramifard
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in standard Reinforcement Learning (RL) where a single final outcome is used to assign credit to all actions in an agentic system.
TRIAGE proposes a novel 'role-typed credit assignment framework' to provide a more nuanced signal for environment-facing actions (e.g., searches, clicks).
This framework aims to solve issues where standard methods incorrectly penalize useful exploration or reinforce redundant actions.

stories.title: FedLAB: Traceable Semantic Codebooks for Federated Multimodal Graph Foundation Learning
stories.author: Guoren Wang
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of training multimodal graph foundation models when data is decentralized due to privacy constraints.
Proposes 'FedLAB,' a method for federated multimodal graph foundation learning.
Focuses on using 'Traceable Semantic Codebooks' to enable knowledge sharing across distributed clients.

stories.title: CoMet: Context and Multiplicity Decomposition for Multimodal Uncertainty Estimation
stories.author: Olga Russakovsky
bullet_points: The paper addresses the critical and difficult problem of uncertainty estimation in AI models, particularly Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
It introduces 'CoMet,' a method designed for Context and Multiplicity Decomposition to better quantify uncertainty.
Uncertainty in MLLMs is complex, stemming from diverse sources and the relationships between different modalities.

stories.title: Introspective Coupling: Self-Explanation Training Tracks Behavioral Change Despite Fixed Supervision
stories.author: Belinda Z. Li
bullet_points: The research investigates whether training Language Models (LMs) to generate explanations leads to genuine introspection or just superficial imitation.
The study uses counterfactual behavior on modified inputs as a form of supervision for the explanation training.
Findings suggest that LMs can track behavioral change even when trained on fixed counterfactual explanations from earlier or similar models.

stories.title: QVal: Cheaply Evaluating Dense Supervision Signals for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
stories.author: Matthias Bethge
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of sparse rewards in long-horizon LLM agent tasks.
Proposes QVal, a method for cheaply evaluating dense supervision signals for intermediate actions.
Focuses on improving guidance for LLM agents beyond relying solely on final outcome rewards.

stories.title: When LLMs Read Tables Carelessly: Measuring and Reducing Data Referencing Errors
stories.author: Huzefa Rangwala
bullet_points: LLMs exhibit data referencing errors (DREs) when processing tables, even when they understand the table structure.
DREs are critical because they compromise the correctness of intermediate reasoning steps, not just the final answer.
This work provides the first systematic, large-scale evaluation of these tabular data referencing errors.

stories.title: Generative Skill Composition for LLM Agents
stories.author: Tianlong Chen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the challenge of selecting appropriate skill compositions for LLM agents, which are used to solve complex tasks.
Skills are defined as modular packages of procedural knowledge for specialized actions (e.g., environment setup, testing, refactoring).
Existing methods for skill composition selection are categorized, indicating a focus on improving agent orchestration.

stories.title: Scalable Behaviour Cloning on Browser Using via Skill Distillation
stories.author: Qinhuai Na
bullet_points: Proposes using human web browsing behavior as a scalable source of reusable skills for AI agents.
Argues that the primary limitation for browser agents is decision-making under incomplete information, not low-level operations.
Introduces 'Skill Distillation' to leverage implicit knowledge present in human interaction traces.

stories.title: DigitalCoach: Communication and Grounding Gaps in Human and Agentic Computer Use Coaching
stories.author: Amy Pavel
bullet_points: Introduces 'DigitalCoach,' a multimodal dataset designed to study computer use coaching.
The dataset comprises 72 expert-novice coaching sessions, including screen and input event recordings across five applications.
The research aims to evaluate the capability of state-of-the-art AI models to effectively teach human software usage.

stories.title: MECoBench: A Systematic Study of Multimodal Agent Collaboration in Embodied Environments
stories.author: Zhongyu Wei
bullet_points: Introduces MECoBench, a new benchmark for evaluating multimodal agent collaboration in embodied environments.
Addresses the gap in understanding how current Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) collaborate in visually grounded settings.
The benchmark tests diverse real-world tasks across two cooperation structures and three collaboration modes.

stories.title: Claude Code is steganographically marking requests
stories.author: kirushik
bullet_points: Claude Code, an AI model, is reportedly embedding hidden data within its requests.
The technique used is steganography, meaning data is concealed within seemingly innocuous information.
This raises potential concerns regarding privacy, data leakage, or model behavior transparency.

stories.title: Godot will no longer accept AI-authored code contributions
stories.author: pjmlp
bullet_points: The Godot game engine is implementing a policy change regarding code contributions.
The engine will cease accepting code that is solely authored by Artificial Intelligence.
This signals a growing push within open-source development to maintain human authorship standards.

stories.title: The first early human eggs from stem cells
stories.author: dsr12
bullet_points: Represents a significant step in regenerative medicine and reproductive biology.
Involves the creation of early human eggs using stem cell technology.
This research has broad implications for future fertility treatments and understanding human development.
